title: To Minerva
content:
My temples throb, my pulses boil,
        I'm sick of Song and Ode and Ballad - 
    So Thyrsis, take the midnight oil,
        And pour it on a lobster salad.

    My brain is dull, my sight is foul,
        I cannot write a verse, or read - 
    Then Pallas, take away thine Owl,
        And let us have a Lark instead.

                            Thomas Hood.
meaning:
Thomas Hood's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "To Minerva"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
